commands.repair_reason: - [P2] Requires owner approval for compatibility-sensitive public SDK and cross-channel delivery behavior, not an automatable code repair.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain defect was found; the diff adds no dependencies or workflows, and terminal fallback text is opt-in, redacted, and bounded. (sha=48fb0e66cf11a4236782c255226c211116d7cd5b)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds opt-in terminal fallback metadata and safe terminal reply handling across agent-core/tool plugins, embedded runner delivery evidence, docs, Docker build cleanup, and focused tests.

PR surface: Source +2279, Tests +6209, Docs +79, Generated 0, Other +1. Total +8568 across 64 files.

Reproducibility: Partly yes: current main lacks the terminal fallback resolver and public plugin metadata path, and the PR body supplies after-fix live Discord output. I did not run a failing current-main live reproduction, so this is source-reproducible rather than fully reproduced here.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Requires owner approval for compatibility-sensitive public SDK and cross-channel delivery behavior, not an automatable code repair.

target.title: fix: surface safe terminal tool fallbacks
target.body:
## Summary

- Add opt-in terminal fallback metadata through agent-core, session tool definitions, subscribe handling, and the embedded runner.
- Surface safe terminal summaries for tool-loop and incomplete-turn exits, while keeping undeclared tool output neutral instead of leaking raw results.
- Declare structured fallbacks for `cron` and `web_fetch`, and keep Docker image builds from carrying stale `dist` output.
- Tighten delivery/side-effect evidence for channel replies, source-reply deliveries, context-engine pairing, compaction ownership, heartbeat lifecycle handling, and tool-plugin result preservation.

## Contract note

- `terminalSummary` and `terminalResultFallback` are intentionally public tool metadata for safe terminal presentation.
- Tools opt in explicitly; undeclared raw tool output is not surfaced as a fallback.
- Generic tool activity, accepted child-session spawns, messaging sends, cron additions, and replay-invalid aborts continue to suppress fallback model replay so side effects are not duplicated.
- `defineToolPlugin` preserves intentional helper-created tool results while continuing to JSON-wrap bare text-shaped objects with `content` and `details` fields.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/tool-plugin.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.tools.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.runreplyagent.e2e.test.ts -- --reporter=dot`
- `pnpm check:docs`
- `pnpm lint --threads=8`
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base upstream/main --codex-bin /Users/jason/.codex/openclaw-pr-autopilot/bin/codex-persist-sessions --model codex=gpt-5.5 --thinking codex=high`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Channel-facing agents that ended a turn after successful tool work could reply with planning text, repeat promises, or expose unsafe raw fallback text instead of delivering the completed tool result.

Real environment tested: A live Discord-backed OpenClaw stack using the patched image and a connected bot. Identifying stack, bot, channel, image, and run IDs are intentionally omitted from this public PR.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Sent live prompts that exercised a scheduler/cron path and a `web_fetch` tool-loop timeout path through the running gateway.

Evidence after fix:

Redacted live channel output, scheduler path:

```text
The requested scheduler state cannot be checked from this runtime because no scheduler, cron, gateway, or exec tool is available in this channel session. I cannot claim a live cron check without that tool.
```

Redacted live channel output, `web_fetch` terminal fallback path:

```text
URL: https://example.com
Final URL: https://example.com
Status: 200
Title: Example Domain
Cached: true
Truncated: false
```

Observed result after fix: The channel received user-visible results for both proof cases. The `web_fetch` fallback included URL, final URL, status, title, cache state, and truncation state, with no raw unsafe tool dump. The scheduler path returned a direct capability blocker instead of a promise to perform an unavailable check.

What was not tested: Broad cross-channel E2E beyond Discord was not rerun. One unrelated live runtime metadata drift was observed during proof and is omitted here because it is not part of this patch.
target.author: fuller-stack-dev

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

commands.author: fuller-stack-dev

target.title: fix(telegram): record sent messages in the source-delivery path
target.body:
## Summary

- The Telegram source-delivery path (`deliverReplies` in `extensions/telegram/src/bot/delivery.replies.ts`) sends via `sendTelegramText` / raw media APIs and emits the `message_sent` hook, but never appends to the sent-message ledger.
- The canonical `sendMessageTelegram` (`extensions/telegram/src/send.ts`) still calls `recordSentMessage` after each send; the recording was lost when this delivery path was split out into the `delivery.*` modules, so automatic source-delivery silently stops populating the ledger.
- Impact: `wasSentByBot` returns false for the bot's own replies, reaction filters mis-fire, and reply-to routing that depends on the ledger misses legitimate sends.
- Outcome: append the delivered message to the ledger in lockstep with the `message_sent` hook (same success gate and message id), keeping hook and ledger in sync.
- Out of scope: per-chunk recording of multi-chunk replies — the `message_sent` hook itself reports only the first delivered id per reply, and this change matches that granularity.
- Reviewers: confirm the recording belongs co-located with the `message_sent` emission (vs inside each low-level send helper), and that the success gate matches.

## Linked context

Closes #92242

Related: `extensions/telegram/src/send.ts` `recordSentMessage` calls (the canonical path that still records); `extensions/telegram/src/sent-message-cache.ts` (`recordSentMessage` / `wasSentByBot`).

Requested by a maintainer or owner? No — filed against open issue #92242.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: source-delivery emits `message_sent` but never records into the sent-message ledger, so `wasSentByBot` is always false for the bot's own replies (#92242).
- Real environment tested: Linux, Node 24, this repo checkout. Ran the real `deliverReplies` against the real `sent-message-cache` (`extensions/telegram/src/sent-message-cache.ts`) with a stub Telegram Bot whose `api.sendMessage` returns a `message_id`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node node_modules/tsx/dist/cli.mjs proof-92242.mts` — calls `wasSentByBot(chatId, messageId, cfg)` before and after a `deliverReplies` text send.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): copied live console output (stdout) from the real delivery + ledger:

```
ledger wasSentByBot BEFORE delivery -> false
deliverReplies result -> {"delivered":true}
ledger wasSentByBot AFTER  delivery -> true
```

- Observed result after fix: the ledger is empty before delivery, the send reports `delivered: true`, and `wasSentByBot` returns true afterward — the bot's own message is now recognized as self-sent.
- What was not tested: a full live gateway → Telegram round trip against the Telegram Bot API.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: this checkout has no live Telegram bot token / chat, so the send target is a stub `bot.api.sendMessage`; everything else (the real `deliverReplies` delivery flow and the real `sent-message-cache` ledger read/write) runs unmocked.
- Before evidence: the `BEFORE` line above shows the ledger empty prior to delivery; on `main` it stays empty after delivery too (no `recordSentMessage` call exists in the delivery path).

## Tests and validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/telegram/src/bot/delivery.records-sent-message.test.ts` → 2 passed (new file: records on success, skips when nothing delivered).
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/telegram/src/bot/delivery.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot/delivery.resolve-media-retry.test.ts` → 195 passed (regression).
- `oxfmt --check` clean on both changed files.
- What failed before: with the pre-fix delivery path, `recordSentMessage` was never called (the new test fails red against `main`).

## Risk checklist

- Did user-visible behavior change? Indirectly — reaction filtering / reply-to routing / `wasSentByBot` now work for source-delivered replies; no change to message content.
- Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? No.
- Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? No.
- Highest-risk area: double-recording or recording on failure.
- Mitigation: the call is gated on the same `progress.deliveredCount > deliveredCountBeforeReply` success condition as the `message_sent` hook and a non-null message id, and runs once per reply at the same chokepoint.

## Current review state

- Next action: maintainer review.
- Waiting on: review; no known author blockers.
- Bot/reviewer comments addressed: none yet.
target.author: tangtaizong666
